title: UTILITY SUPPORT - 211info
text: COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT UTILITY SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with electricity, natural gas, oil, propane, wood or pellets, and select water/wastewater bills. Funds are limited, and assistance is not guaranteed. May not be eligible for funding if recently received assistance and have not completed the waiting period to be eligible again. Eligibility: All applicants must have a gross monthly income that is at or below 60% of the Oregon State median income for their household size Heating Assistance: October 1-April 15: Age 60 and older, adults with disabilities, and medically fragile households November 1-April 15: Households with children age 0-6 December 1-April 15: All income-eligible households Cooling Assistance: May 1-September 15: Age 60 and older, adults with disabilities, and medically fragile households June 1-September 15: Open to the general public Water Assistance: Customers of Sunrise Water Authority Hours: Monday-Thursday 7am-6pm Email Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or visit website for more information and Utility Support Application Request form FEES: None DOCUMENTS: Gross income verification for previous calendar month for all adults age 18 and older in the household, copy of current utility bill (or receipt for wood, propane, oil, or pellets), proof of legal name and current physical address, legal ID for all adults age 18 and older in the household, official documentation to verify SSN (if applicant has SSN) LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, Russian AREA SERVED: Clackamas County SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Located at the intersection of Kaen and Beavercreek Roads, at the top of the hill in Oregon City. On TriMet bus line 33 with sign Clackamas Community College/CCC. Services: Other Services Offered At This Location: HOUSING/SHELTER - CLACKAMAS COUNTY COORDINATED HOUSING ACCESS If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. No Mapping Information Available
